title: jit init default ruleset scaffolding and existing-repo migration
description:
Scaffold a default `.jit/rules.toml` on `jit init` and migrate existing repos' legacy config into rules when init is re-run.

## Background

Repos obtain their checks from `rules.toml`: new repos via `init`, existing repos by re-running `init`, which performs the one-time migration. Stale configs must not break before migration. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` (step 9) and decision record sections 8.2 and 8.4.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `jit init` scaffolds a default `.jit/rules.toml` reproducing the default checks.
- [ ] [hard] Re-running `jit init` on an existing repo migrates `[validation]` and `[namespaces].{values,pattern,required}` config into `rules.toml` and strips the old enforcement keys.
- [ ] [hard] A pre-parse `toml::Value` scan in `JitConfig::load` warns when deprecated keys are present (top-level `validation`; nested `namespaces.*.{values,pattern,required}`) and prompts to re-run `jit init`; no `deny_unknown_fields` is added, so stale configs never hard-error.
- [ ] [hard] Existing repos obtain defaults without silently losing validation; a test covers an old config carrying removed keys (it warns, parses, and does not error).

## Notes

Anchors: init path `main.rs:272` + `executor.init` in `commands/mod.rs`; config load `config.rs:405-430`, `config_manager.rs:51-53`. There is currently no `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`, so serde ignores unknown fields by default.
